profile.summary: Krishna Teja Reddy Suram is a Data Science graduate with experience in data analytics, machine learning, SQL, Python, retrieval-augmented generation systems, healthcare AI, dashboards, ETL workflows, and applied AI product development.
profile.current_status: He completed his Master's in Data Science at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County (UMBC) in December 2025 and is targeting Data Analyst, Data Scientist, ML Engineer, and AI Engineer roles.
profile.availability: Open to data, AI, analytics, and machine learning opportunities, collaborations, projects, and professional discussions.

education.degree: Master of Science in Data Science
notes: Built a strong foundation in data analysis, machine learning, data visualization, statistics, SQL, ETL concepts, and large language model applications.
Completed coursework and projects involving data management, predictive modeling, analytics, and applied AI systems.

education.degree: Bachelor's in Electronics and Communication Engineering
education.institution: Sri Venkateswara University College of Engineering
notes: Undergraduate background in engineering, systems thinking, and technical problem solving.

experience.summary: Worked on applied data science, analytics, ETL, machine learning, and AI projects across healthcare, NLP, computer vision, and business analysis.
details: Built end-to-end project workflows involving data extraction, preprocessing, analysis, modeling, evaluation, and deployment.
Used SQL, Excel, Python, and dashboarding tools for structured data analysis and reporting.
Worked on ETL-related concepts and data management through coursework and projects.

experience.summary: Held multiple leadership and mentoring roles during undergraduate studies.
details: Served as General Secretary for the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering.
Taught M1 classes to first-year undergraduates.
Participated in the Programming Club and Photography Club.

experience.summary: Has a strong background in tutoring, mentoring, and student support.
details: Interested in tutoring and academic support roles.
Comfortable helping others learn technical and academic concepts.

projects.name: Healthcare Analytics Pipeline using MIMIC-III
projects.summary: Built a healthcare analytics pipeline to predict in-hospital mortality and 30-day readmission using MIMIC-III data with structured and unstructured features, followed by deployment in Streamlit.
highlights: Worked with clinical data and machine learning pipelines.
Combined structured and unstructured healthcare information.
Focused on predictive modeling for patient outcomes.
Deployed the solution using Streamlit.

projects.name: Reddit FAQ Chatbot
projects.summary: Built a domain-specific Reddit FAQ chatbot using retrieval-augmented generation with LlamaIndex, ChromaDB, Gemini, safety filtering, and evaluation components.
highlights: Designed a knowledge-grounded chatbot architecture.
Used retrieval and vector-based storage concepts.
Included safety filtering and evaluation logic.
Focused on domain-specific question answering.

projects.name: COVID-19 Chest X-ray CNN Model
projects.summary: Built an AlexNet-inspired convolutional neural network for COVID-19 chest X-ray classification and deployed it through a Flask-based application for real-time inference.
highlights: Worked on image-based medical classification.
Used CNN architecture inspired by AlexNet.
Built a Flask deployment for inference.

projects.name: Bangalore Home Price Regression
projects.summary: Built a regression pipeline to predict Bangalore home prices using Kaggle data, including data cleaning, feature engineering, location dimensionality reduction, and domain-based outlier removal.
highlights: Worked on regression modeling and feature engineering.
Computed price per square foot and engineered useful features.
Reduced dimensionality on location data.
Removed outliers using domain logic.

projects.name: Healthcare Prediction using Decision Tree and SVM
projects.summary: Worked on healthcare prediction using Decision Tree and SVM models with confusion matrix analysis, ROC plotting, hyperparameter tuning, and SHAP-based interpretation.
highlights: Compared traditional machine learning models.
Evaluated models using classification metrics and ROC analysis.
Performed hyperparameter tuning and interpretability analysis.

projects.name: Statistics and Data Analysis Work
projects.summary: Worked on statistics and data analysis assignments involving hypothesis testing, ANOVA, regression, confidence intervals, and chi-square tests.
highlights: Applied core statistical methods.
Connected analytics to inference and decision-making.

faq.answer: Krishna Teja Reddy Suram is a Data Science graduate from UMBC with experience in data analytics, machine learning, SQL, Python, RAG systems, and applied AI projects across healthcare, NLP, and computer vision.

Krishna completed his Master's in Data Science at the University of Maryland, Baltimore County in December 2025. He also holds an undergraduate degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering from Sri Venkateswara University College of Engineering.
His skills include Python, SQL, JavaScript, HTML, CSS, machine learning, data analysis, data visualization, Excel, Tableau, Power BI, Flask, FastAPI, Streamlit, RAG systems, LlamaIndex, and ChromaDB.
